status_notes: Initial research from AWS DynamoDB documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference and live environment validation.
notes: Managed NoSQL key-value and document database. Serverless, fully managed. Security includes encryption, IAM fine-grained access, VPC endpoints.

match_nodes.description: Encryption at rest for DynamoDB tables

data.description: All DynamoDB tables are encrypted at rest. Three key management options available. Cannot be disabled once a table is created.

options.name: AWS Owned Key
options.description: Default. AWS owns and manages the key. No additional cost. No CloudTrail logging of key usage.

options.name: AWS Managed Key (aws/dynamodb)
options.description: AWS managed KMS key. CloudTrail logging available. No additional KMS cost.

options.name: Customer Managed Key
options.description: Customer managed KMS key. Full audit trail, key rotation control, and ability to revoke access. KMS API costs apply.

default.note: Default encryption uses an AWS owned key at no additional cost.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use a customer managed key for sensitive workloads requiring audit trail and key rotation control.

match_nodes.description: IAM conditions for item-level and attribute-level access control

data.description: Use IAM policy conditions for item-level and attribute-level access control. Restrict access based on partition key values (leading keys) and limit which attributes a user can read or write. No database-level access control — all access is through IAM.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use IAM condition keys (dynamodb:LeadingKeys, dynamodb:Attributes) to implement least-privilege access at the item and attribute level.

audit.note: Review IAM policies attached to roles/users that access DynamoDB. Check for condition keys dynamodb:LeadingKeys and dynamodb:Attributes.

match_nodes.description: Gateway endpoint for private DynamoDB access from VPC

data.description: Gateway endpoint for private DynamoDB access from within a VPC. Traffic stays within the AWS network. No internet gateway or NAT device required. Gateway endpoints are free.

default.note: VPC endpoints must be explicitly created.

data.vendor_recommendation: Create a gateway endpoint for DynamoDB in every VPC that has workloads accessing DynamoDB.

data.description: Continuous backups with point-in-time recovery. Allows restoring to any point in the last 35 days. Creates a new table on restore (does not overwrite existing).

default.note: Point-in-time recovery is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable point-in-time recovery on all production tables.

data.description: Prevent accidental table deletion. When enabled, the table cannot be deleted until deletion protection is explicitly disabled.

default.note: Deletion protection is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable deletion protection on all production tables.

audit.console: DynamoDB -> Tables -> {table} -> Additional settings -> Deletion protection

match_nodes.description: Identify most accessed keys and throttled items

data.description: Identify the most frequently accessed and throttled keys in a DynamoDB table. Security relevant for detecting abuse patterns, identifying hot partitions, and investigating unusual access patterns.

default.note: Contributor Insights is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Contributor Insights on production tables to detect access anomalies and abuse patterns.
